summary: Fix install's yes-path so one install/emit run leaves the lock's fingerprint rows matching the final, placement-inclusive bytes — no second run needed to converge.

edit.description: run_represented's yes-path (fn at ~line 373) calls drift::emit_program (writes lock.toml's emit_hash/source_hash rows from pre-placement bytes) then evaluate_placements (~line 514, mutates each emit-owned target's bytes: managed-by note, schema modeline) with no re-stamp step after — so drift::config_stale reports a fingerprint mismatch on the harness's own first-install output. Fix so a single yes-path run leaves the lock's fingerprints matching final bytes. Verified fix shape: emit_one (src/drift.rs ~line 646) already folds on-disk placement lines into its render+hash, so re-invoking drift::emit_program(dry_run:false) after evaluate_placements finishes (when !dry_run) re-derives correct fingerprints and rewrites the lock via its existing write_rollup path — no drift.rs change strictly required, though build may find a cleaner hook point. Must not regress the dry-run preview (no files exist yet to fingerprint) or emit's double-emit-determinism check.

re_representing_never_re_scaffolds_and_settles_by_the_second_run (~line 519) asserts after_first != after_second (the tree changes again on run two) and documents the two-run settle as accepted — post-fix the first install::run call must already settle, so replace that assertion with an immediate post-first-run fingerprint-consistency check (e.g. temper::drift::config_stale(&temper_dir) is empty right after the first run) and keep the byte-for-byte-no-op-on-repeat assertion. Trim the module doc's 'idempotence — re-running converges (after the note's one settling emit)' bullet (~line 20) to match: converges on the first run.

tests.asserts: immediately after one install::run(&root, &discovery, Represent::Yes, false) call on a fresh unrepresented harness, temper::drift::config_stale(&temper_dir) is empty and a repeat run is a byte-for-byte no-op — never requiring a second run to settle

acceptance: A single `install::run(&root, &discovery, Represent::Yes, false)` (or `temper install --yes`) on a fresh unrepresented harness leaves every emit-owned projection's on-disk bytes matching its lock.toml row's emit_hash/source_hash immediately — drift::config_stale returns no finding and emit --dry-run still reports Unchanged, with no second install/emit invocation required to reach that state.
notes: Confirmed on disk, not the inbox claim alone: run_represented calls emit_program (writes lock fingerprints from pre-placement bytes) then evaluate_placements (mutates the same files' notes/modelines), no re-stamp after. emit_one already folds on-disk placement lines into its hash on a later pass — how tests/install.rs's settle test documents today's two-run settle; the inbox finding (dogfood re-conversion, f684b56) supersedes that acceptance. scoped at f684b56.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
